browse.map.event.tooltip.source: Hover-tooltip line naming the event's source organization on the catalog map. Preserve {name}.
browse.timeline.event.aria: Accessible label for an amber current-event marker on the catalog timeline axis. Preserve {title} — the event's headline.
chat.action.flyingTo: Live-region announcement during a chat-driven camera move. Preserve {coords}. Keep it brief — screen readers read this aloud.
chat.action.navigatingTo: Live-region announcement during a chat-driven navigation. Preserve {label}. Keep it brief.
chat.action.seekingTo: Live-region announcement during a chat-driven date seek. Preserve {date}. Keep it brief.
chat.autoLoad.altHint: Appended to chat.autoLoad.message when alternative datasets are available. Begins with a leading space; preserve the space if your language uses spaces between sentences.
chat.autoLoad.message: Sent when Orbit auto-loads a dataset on the user's first turn. Preserve **{title}** (markdown bold, rendered) and {altHint} (which is either chat.autoLoad.altHint or empty).
chat.fallback.localDev: Shown when the LLM is unreachable in local development. Backticks render as inline code; preserve them around `npm run dev` and `/api`.
chat.fallback.production: Shown when the LLM is unreachable in production. Brief; the user has no way to fix this themselves.
chat.settings.voiceHandsFree.label: Settings label for choosing Orbit's hands-free voice mode (off / push-to-talk / open mic).
chat.settings.voiceHandsFree.off: Hands-free option: disabled — the user taps the mic for each utterance.
chat.settings.voiceHandsFree.openMic: Hands-free option: the mic listens continuously and detects when the user speaks.
chat.settings.voiceHandsFree.pushToTalk: Hands-free option: the mic captures only while a control is held down.
chat.settings.voiceHandsFree.wakeWord: Hands-free option: Orbit listens only after a spoken wake phrase. The exact phrase depends on the wake-word model the operator configured (the built-in default is “Hey Jarvis”), so this label is intentionally phrase-neutral — translate “Wake word” generically.
chat.settings.voiceLang.auto: Default option in Orbit's 'Recognition language' picker. Means voice input/output follows the app's UI language. 'app' = this application.
chat.settings.voiceLang.label: Settings label for the picker that sets which spoken language Orbit listens for and speaks, independent of the UI language.
chat.welcome.hint: Companion line below chat.welcome.intro. Same warm, helpful, non-corporate voice as Orbit. Mentions Browse as a parallel path.
chat.welcome.intro: First-person introduction from Orbit, the AI docent. Voice should be warm and helpful, not corporate. Markdown bold (**Orbit**) renders at runtime; preserve the asterisks.
disclosureBanner.text: First-launch privacy notice. Plain text, no markup. Shown once before any telemetry events fire. Should reassure without being legalistic.
docent.greeting.1: One of three randomly-selected first-message greetings from Orbit when chat opens. First-person, warm, references Earth-science topics. The user has not yet typed anything.
docent.greeting.2: One of three randomly-selected first-message greetings from Orbit. Same constraints as docent.greeting.1.
docent.greeting.3: One of three randomly-selected first-message greetings from Orbit. Same constraints as docent.greeting.1.
docent.help: Orbit's response when the user asks 'what can you do?'. Markdown formatting (bullets, bold) renders at runtime. Mentions specific capabilities — keep examples relevant to Earth-science exploration.
docent.search.fallbackResults: Sent when the user's exact query had no match but a single keyword did. Preserve {query} (the original) and {word} (the matched keyword).
docent.search.multipleMatches: Sent when the user's query matches multiple datasets. Preserve {count}, {query}, **{title}**, {desc}, {qa}. Markdown renders.
docent.search.none: Sent when no datasets match. Preserve {query}. Suggests broad fallback topics; localize the topic suggestions naturally.
docent.search.singleMatch: Sent when one dataset matches the user's query. Preserve **{title}**, {desc}, {qa}. Markdown renders.
docent.system.respondInLanguage: LLM system prompt fragment injected before every chat turn. Preserve <<LOAD:DATASET_ID>> markers verbatim — they are parsed by the runtime tool-call detector. {language} is the user's locale name. The translation IS what the model reads, so phrase it as the instruction you want followed.
docent.system.respondInLanguageReminder: Anchored language reminder injected just before the user's turn. Same constraints as docent.system.respondInLanguage — preserve <<LOAD:...>> markers and {language}.
help.guide.section.exploring: Help-guide HTML blob. Sanitized at runtime, but keep the HTML structure (h3, ul, li, strong, kbd, a tags) intact — translate only the text content. The runtime allowlist rejects script-class tags.
help.guide.section.multiglobe: Help-guide HTML blob. Same constraints as help.guide.section.exploring.
help.guide.section.navigating: Help-guide HTML blob. Same constraints as help.guide.section.exploring.
help.guide.section.orbit: Help-guide HTML blob. Same constraints as help.guide.section.exploring.
help.guide.section.privacy: Help-guide HTML blob. Same constraints as help.guide.section.exploring. The link to /privacy and 'Tools → Privacy' reference must stay accurate.
help.guide.section.shortcuts: Help-guide HTML blob about keyboard shortcuts. Preserve <kbd> tags; the literal key names (Esc, Enter, Space) should localize to whatever your language calls those keys.
help.guide.section.tools: Help-guide HTML blob. Same constraints as help.guide.section.exploring. Preserve the strong-tagged section names (View, Layout, Clear, Orbit settings) but translate them to match the corresponding tools.* strings.
help.guide.section.tours: Help-guide HTML blob. Same constraints as help.guide.section.exploring.
infoPanel.news.viewOnGlobe.aria: Accessible label for the 'View on globe' button on an 'In the news' event card in the dataset info panel. The button navigates the globe to the event — flying to its location and/or seeking the dataset to its time, depending on what the event has. Preserve {title} (the event's headline).
playback.error.dateOutsideRange: Error shown when a seek lands outside the dataset's time range. Preserve {date}, {start}, {end} placeholders verbatim.
playback.seekingTo: Live-region announcement when seeking to a date. Preserve {date}. Brief.
publisher.analytics.controls.rangeDays: Date-range picker option; {days} is a number (7/30/90/365). Keep the {days} placeholder.
publisher.analytics.datasets.loadP50: Table column header; '≈p50' means 'approximately the 50th percentile' — keep the ≈ and p50 notation.
publisher.analytics.datasets.loadP95: Table column header; '≈p95' means 'approximately the 95th percentile' — keep the ≈ and p95 notation.
publisher.analytics.perf.frameP95: Table header; 'frame p95' = the 95th-percentile frame time. Keep the p95 notation.
publisher.chipInput.remove.aria: ARIA label for the × remove-chip button on each entry in a chip-input control. Preserve {value} verbatim — it is the chip's text content, interpolated at runtime so the screen reader announces 'Remove climate' rather than 'Remove'.
publisher.datasetDetail.backToList: Label for the back-arrow link at the top of /publish/datasets/:id. Navigates the publisher back to the dataset list. The visible character is '←' followed by this label.
publisher.datasetDetail.field.attributio: Field label on the dataset detail page for the dataset's attribution text (the citation-style 'Visualization by NOAA/SOS' line).
publisher.datasetDetail.field.captionRef: Field label for the closed-captions asset reference (VTT). Single word.
publisher.datasetDetail.field.citation: Field label for a structured citation string (BibTeX-style or freeform).
publisher.datasetDetail.field.createdAt: Field label for the dataset's row creation timestamp. Single word.
publisher.datasetDetail.field.dataRef: Field label for the primary asset reference — the URI-style scheme that points at the actual data (`vimeo:`, `stream:`, `r2:`, `url:`). Rendered in a monospace cell.
publisher.datasetDetail.field.doi: Field label for the dataset's DOI when set. Acronym; render verbatim.
publisher.datasetDetail.field.endTime: Field label for the dataset's time-range end. ISO 8601 timestamp.
publisher.datasetDetail.field.format: Field label for the dataset's MIME-style format (`video/mp4`, `image/png`, `tour/json`). Same key text as `publisher.datasets.col.format` but rendered as a field label rather than a column header.
publisher.datasetDetail.field.id: Field label for the dataset's ULID primary key. Rendered in a monospace cell.
publisher.datasetDetail.field.legacyId: Field label for the dataset's legacy SOS identifier (`INTERNAL_SOS_*`) when imported via the bulk SOS snapshot. NULL for publisher-created rows.
publisher.datasetDetail.field.legendRef: Field label for the legend image reference. Single word.
publisher.datasetDetail.field.licenseSpd: Field label for the dataset's SPDX license identifier (`CC0-1.0`, `CC-BY-4.0`).
publisher.datasetDetail.field.licenseSta: Field label for free-text license text when the license isn't an SPDX-listed one.
publisher.datasetDetail.field.licenseUrl: Field label for the canonical URL of the dataset's license.
publisher.datasetDetail.field.organizati: Field label for the dataset's publishing organisation (e.g., 'NOAA/PMEL'). Same key text as `publisher.me.field.organization` — separate key so the detail card can tune the label independently.
publisher.datasetDetail.field.period: Field label for the dataset's sampling period (ISO 8601 duration: `P1D`, `PT1H`).
publisher.datasetDetail.field.publishedA: Field label for the dataset's first-published timestamp. NULL until the publisher transitions the row out of draft.
publisher.datasetDetail.field.publisherI: Field label for the ULID of the publisher who owns the row. Rendered in a monospace cell.
publisher.datasetDetail.field.retractedA: Field label for the dataset's retraction timestamp. NULL when the row is not retracted.
publisher.datasetDetail.field.rightsHold: Field label for the rights-holder text (`U.S. Government`, an organisation, or a person).
publisher.datasetDetail.field.runTourOnL: Field label for the optional tour reference the dataset auto-loads (`tours.id`). NULL when none.
publisher.datasetDetail.field.startTime: Field label for the dataset's time-range start. ISO 8601 timestamp.
publisher.datasetDetail.field.thumbnailR: Field label for the thumbnail image reference. Single word.
publisher.datasetDetail.field.updatedAt: Field label for the dataset row's last-modified timestamp.
publisher.datasetDetail.field.visibility: Field label for the dataset's visibility (`public`, `federated`, `restricted`, `private`).
publisher.datasetDetail.field.websiteLin: Field label for an external web page about the dataset (e.g., a NOAA project page).
publisher.datasetDetail.loading: Live-region status text shown while the dataset detail fetch is in flight. Brief.
publisher.datasetDetail.notFound: Error message on the dataset detail page when the row doesn't exist OR isn't visible to the calling publisher (the API returns 404 for both to avoid leaking other publishers' draft IDs). Pairs with the back-arrow link at the top of the page.
publisher.datasetDetail.section.abstract: Section heading for the abstract card on the dataset detail page. Single word.
publisher.datasetDetail.section.assets: Section heading for the assets card on the dataset detail page — data reference, thumbnail, legend, captions, website link.
publisher.datasetDetail.section.identity: Section heading for the identity card on the dataset detail page — ULID, legacy ID, format, visibility, organization, publisher ID. Single word.
publisher.datasetDetail.section.licensin: Section heading for the licensing card on the dataset detail page — SPDX license, license URL, statement, attribution, rights holder, DOI, citation.
publisher.datasetDetail.section.lifecycl: Section heading for the lifecycle card on the dataset detail page — created/updated/published/retracted timestamps and the dataset's time-range fields. Single word.
publisher.datasetForm.action.cancel: Label for the Cancel button on the dataset create / edit form. Navigates back to the list. Single word.
publisher.datasetForm.action.edit: Label for the toggle button above the abstract textarea when the form is showing the rendered markdown preview. Clicking switches back to the editable source view. Single word.
publisher.datasetForm.action.preview: Label for the toggle button above the abstract textarea when the form is showing the editable source. Clicking switches to the rendered markdown preview. Single word.
publisher.datasetForm.action.saveDraft: Label for the primary Save draft button on the dataset create / edit form. Persists the row with `published_at` still NULL.
publisher.datasetForm.action.saving: Replaces the Save draft button label while the POST/PUT is in flight. Brief.
publisher.datasetForm.datetime.date: Sub-label above the date half of a Start time / End time picker on the dataset form. The picker is split into two inputs (date + time) so the publisher can see both halves clearly; this label sits above the `<input type="date">` half.
publisher.datasetForm.datetime.time: Sub-label above the time half of a Start time / End time picker on the dataset form. The `(24h)` suffix tells the publisher to use 24-hour clock format; preserve it in the translation since the time input accepts 24-hour values regardless of locale.
publisher.datasetForm.deferredHint: Internal-context paragraph at the bottom of the 3pc/B form explaining which fields are still deferred to later sub-phases. Will be removed in 3pc/C once those fields ship. Not for end users.
publisher.datasetForm.field.abstract: Field label on the dataset form for the abstract — a longer description of the dataset, supports markdown. Sits above the textarea.
publisher.datasetForm.field.attribution: Field label on the dataset form for the attribution text (e.g., 'Visualization by NOAA/PMEL'). Same surface as `publisher.datasetDetail.field.attribution` but the form may want a different label length.
publisher.datasetForm.field.citation: Field label on the dataset form for the citation text. Plain text; freeform.
publisher.datasetForm.field.doi: Field label on the dataset form for the DOI. Acronym; render verbatim.
publisher.datasetForm.field.endTime: Field label on the dataset form for the end of the dataset's time range. Renders as an HTML datetime-local picker; the publisher enters local time, the form converts to ISO 8601 UTC on submit.
publisher.datasetForm.field.format: Field legend on the dataset form for the Format radio group (video/image/tour).
publisher.datasetForm.field.keywords: Field label on the dataset form for the keywords chip input — short search-aware labels the docent / browse UI uses for filtering.
publisher.datasetForm.field.licenseSpdx: Field label on the dataset form for the SPDX license identifier. Acronym; render verbatim.
publisher.datasetForm.field.licenseState: Field label on the dataset form for the free-text license statement (used when no SPDX identifier applies).
publisher.datasetForm.field.licenseUrl: Field label on the dataset form for the canonical license URL.
publisher.datasetForm.field.organization: Field label on the dataset form for the publishing organisation (e.g., 'NOAA/PMEL'). Same surface as `publisher.datasetDetail.field.organization`; kept as a separate key in case the form label needs to differ.
publisher.datasetForm.field.period: Field label on the dataset form for the dataset's sampling period (ISO 8601 duration: P1D, PT1H, P1M).
publisher.datasetForm.field.rightsHolder: Field label on the dataset form for the rights-holder text.
publisher.datasetForm.field.slug: Field label on the dataset form for the URL slug. Auto-derived from title; editable. Single word.
publisher.datasetForm.field.startTime: Field label on the dataset form for the start of the dataset's time range. Renders as an HTML datetime-local picker; same conversion as the End time field.
publisher.datasetForm.field.tags: Field label on the dataset form for the tags chip input — free-form labels distinct from keywords (e.g., 'demo', 'featured', 'internal').
publisher.datasetForm.field.title: Field label on the dataset form for the dataset's display title. Single word.
publisher.datasetForm.field.visibility: Field legend on the dataset form for the Visibility radio group (public/federated/restricted/private).
publisher.datasetForm.format.imageJpeg: Format radio option for image/jpeg datasets.
publisher.datasetForm.format.imagePng: Format radio option for image/png datasets.
publisher.datasetForm.format.imageWebp: Format radio option for image/webp datasets.
publisher.datasetForm.format.tour: Format radio option for tour/json (SOS-style guided tour) datasets.
publisher.datasetForm.format.video: Format radio option for video/mp4 datasets. Default selection on a new draft.
publisher.datasetForm.headingEdit: H1 heading at the top of the edit-existing-dataset form. Mirrors `publisher.datasetForm.headingNew` shape. Single short phrase.
publisher.datasetForm.headingNew: H1 heading at the top of the create-new-dataset form. Single short phrase.
publisher.datasetForm.help.abstract: Helper text below the Abstract textarea listing the supported markdown features. Should mention `**bold**`, `*italic*`, lists, code, links — preserve these verbatim as literal markdown syntax in your translation, since the publisher will type them. Header markers `##` / `###` are also literal.
publisher.datasetForm.help.keywords: Helper text below the Keywords chip input. Documents the Enter / comma → add gesture and the 20-keyword / 40-character caps from the server validator. Translators should keep the gesture wording matching whatever Enter / comma idiom feels natural in their locale.
publisher.datasetForm.help.licenseSpdx: Helper text below the License (SPDX) input. Mentions the standard SPDX-license-list identifier format and that one of SPDX or License statement must be set before the dataset can transition from draft to published.
publisher.datasetForm.help.period: Helper text below the Period input listing common ISO 8601 duration examples. Preserve the literal P1D / PT1H / P1M / PT15M tokens verbatim — they are not localisable.
publisher.datasetForm.help.slug: Helper text below the Slug field explaining the auto-derivation behaviour and the validation rule (lowercase letters, digits, hyphens; must start with a letter).
publisher.datasetForm.help.tags: Helper text below the Tags chip input. Same Enter / comma gesture as keywords. Mentions the 'free-form, distinct from keywords' purpose so the publisher knows when to use each field.
publisher.datasetForm.help.timeRange: Helper text at the bottom of the Time range card. Two messages: (a) the local→UTC conversion the form applies on submit, and (b) the 'both or neither, start ≤ end' rule the validator enforces. Translators may rephrase but preserve those two facts.
publisher.datasetForm.placeholder.abstra: Placeholder text inside the Abstract textarea on a fresh form. Shown as a hint; cleared on focus. The backticks around `sst-anomaly-monthly` render as inline code in the eventual public detail page; preserve them to model proper markdown usage to the publisher.
publisher.datasetForm.placeholder.keywor: Placeholder text inside the Keywords chip input on a fresh form. Shows a comma-separated example to hint at the gesture. Translate the example tokens into realistic locale-appropriate values.
publisher.datasetForm.placeholder.licens: Placeholder text inside the License statement textarea. Shown as a hint; cleared on focus.
publisher.datasetForm.placeholder.tags: Placeholder text inside the Tags chip input on a fresh form. Same gesture-modelling purpose as the Keywords placeholder.
publisher.datasetForm.placeholder.title: Placeholder text inside the Title field on a fresh form. Shown as a hint; cleared on focus. Should look like a plausible real dataset title.
publisher.datasetForm.preview.empty: Shown in place of the rendered preview when the abstract textarea is empty / whitespace-only. Brief; invites the publisher to switch back to the Edit view.
publisher.datasetForm.requiredAria: Screen-reader announcement for the asterisk that marks required form fields. Read aloud as 'required' so the visual `*` has audible meaning.
publisher.datasetForm.section.abstract: Section heading on the dataset form for the abstract markdown card. Single word.
publisher.datasetForm.section.categoriza: Section heading on the dataset form for the categorization card — keywords, tags. Single word.
publisher.datasetForm.section.identity: Section heading on the dataset form grouping the identity-tier fields (title, slug, format, visibility). Single word.
publisher.datasetForm.section.licensing: Section heading on the dataset form for the licensing card — SPDX, license URL, statement, attribution, rights holder, DOI, citation. Two words.
publisher.datasetForm.section.timeRange: Section heading on the dataset form for the time-range card — start time, end time, period. Two words.
publisher.datasetForm.visibility.federat: Visibility radio option meaning 'this dataset is shared with federation peers'.
publisher.datasetForm.visibility.private: Visibility radio option meaning 'only the publisher and staff can see this dataset'.
publisher.datasetForm.visibility.public: Visibility radio option meaning 'anyone can see this dataset'. The default for staff publishers.
publisher.datasetForm.visibility.restric: Visibility radio option meaning 'visible only to specific grants the publisher issues'. Phase 5 feature; ships earlier in the form so the field is complete.
publisher.datasets.col.format: Column heading on the publisher's dataset table — shows the dataset's MIME-style format (`video/mp4`, `image/png`, `tour/json`, …). Single word; tight column.
publisher.datasets.col.slug: Column heading on the publisher's dataset table — shows the dataset's URL slug (e.g., `sst-anomaly-2026-04`). Single word; rendered in a monospace cell.
publisher.datasets.col.status: Column heading on the publisher's dataset table — shows the lifecycle badge (Draft / Published / Retracted). Single word.
publisher.datasets.col.title: Column heading on the publisher's dataset table — the dataset's title, rendered as a link to the detail page. Single word.
publisher.datasets.col.updated: Column heading on the publisher's dataset table — shows the dataset's last-modified date (`updated_at` server field, localized via Intl.DateTimeFormat). Single word.
publisher.datasets.count.one: Pluralised count rendered above the dataset table when exactly one row is displayed. Preserve {count}. CLDR-aware via the i18n plural() helper.
publisher.datasets.count.other: Pluralised count rendered above the dataset table for 0 or 2+ rows. Preserve {count}.
publisher.datasets.empty.drafts: Shown on the Drafts tab when there are no draft datasets. Mentions the CLI and the sub-phase tag '3pc' (the entry form). Internal context, not for end users.
publisher.datasets.empty.published: Shown on the Published tab when there are no published datasets. Brief.
publisher.datasets.empty.retracted: Shown on the Retracted tab when there are no retracted datasets. Brief.
publisher.datasets.loadMore: Visible label on the Load more button at the bottom of the dataset table when more pages are available. Two words; the button paginates via the server's cursor.
publisher.datasets.loading: Live-region status text shown while the first page of datasets is fetching. Brief.
publisher.datasets.loadingMore: Replaces the Load more button label while the next page is fetching. Brief.
publisher.datasets.newDraft: Label for the primary call-to-action button on /publish/datasets that takes the publisher to the create-new-dataset form. The leading '+' is a deliberate visual cue; preserve it.
publisher.datasets.status.draft: Status badge text on a draft dataset row in the table (not the tab — the tab is `tab.drafts`). Single word.
publisher.datasets.status.published: Status badge text on a published dataset row. Single word.
publisher.datasets.status.retracted: Status badge text on a retracted dataset row. Single word.
publisher.datasets.tab.drafts: Tab label for the Drafts filter on /publish/datasets. Routes to ?status=draft. Keep short.
publisher.datasets.tab.published: Tab label for the Published filter on /publish/datasets. Routes to ?status=published. Keep short.
publisher.datasets.tab.retracted: Tab label for the Retracted filter on /publish/datasets. Routes to ?status=retracted. Keep short.
publisher.datasets.tabs.aria: ARIA label for the lifecycle filter tablist on /publish/datasets. Read by screen readers; not visible text.
publisher.error.detailsSummary: Label on the <details> disclosure widget the portal renders alongside a server-error message. Expanding reveals the HTTP status code and the raw server response body — the operator-debugging affordance. Keep short.
publisher.error.statusLine: Format string for the HTTP status line inside the server-error details disclosure. Preserve {status}.
publisher.events.bulkApprove: Button that approves every dataset pairing scoring ≥90%. Preserve {count} (how many will be approved).
publisher.events.bulkApproved: Confirmation after the bulk-approve action. Preserve {count}.
publisher.events.inferred.date: Lowercase fragment named inside the AI-inferred badge, joined with commas (e.g. 'date, location'). Keep it short and lowercase if your language allows.
publisher.events.inferred.location: Lowercase fragment named inside the AI-inferred badge, joined with commas (e.g. 'date, location'). Keep it short and lowercase if your language allows.
publisher.events.match.compositeAria: Screen-reader label for a dataset's overall match score on the Events review tab. Preserve {value} (a number 0–100).
publisher.events.match.facetAria: Screen-reader label for one match facet (Topic/Time/Geo) on the Events tab. Preserve {facet} (the facet name) and {value} (0–100).
publisher.events.match.facetNa: Screen-reader label when a match facet has no score. Preserve {facet} (the facet name).
publisher.events.match.geoAbbr: Very short visible abbreviation for the Geographic match facet (English 'G' for Geo) shown on a compact badge tag. Keep it 1–2 characters.
publisher.events.match.timeAbbr: Very short visible abbreviation for the Time (temporal) match facet (English 'Ti') shown on a compact badge tag. Keep it 1–2 characters.
publisher.events.match.topicAbbr: Very short visible abbreviation for the Topic match facet (English 'T') shown on a compact badge tag. Keep it 1–2 characters.
publisher.events.pairing.approveAria: Screen-reader label for the ✓ button that confirms one dataset pairing. Preserve {title} (the dataset name).
publisher.events.pairing.rejectAria: Screen-reader label for the ✕ button that rejects one dataset pairing. Preserve {title} (the dataset name).
publisher.events.queue.rowAria: Screen-reader label for an event row in the queue. Preserve {title}, {category}, and {count}.
publisher.events.queue.toReview: Sub-line counting an event's dataset pairings to review. Preserve {count}.
publisher.feeds.lastRun.error: Feed-row status line when the last ingest attempt failed. Preserve {detail} (a short technical failure description).
publisher.feeds.lastRun.ok: Feed-row status line after a successful ingest. Preserve {when} (a date-time string).
publisher.feeds.runDone: Summary after running all enabled feeds. Preserve {created}, {refreshed}, and {failed} (counts).
publisher.markdownToolbar.aria: ARIA label for the markdown toolbar above the abstract textarea. Read by screen readers when the user enters the toolbar.
publisher.markdownToolbar.blockquote.ari: ARIA label for the Blockquote button — prepends `> ` to each selected line. Read by screen readers; not visible text.
publisher.markdownToolbar.blockquote.tit: Hover tooltip on the Blockquote button. Explains what the button does. The literal `>` markdown syntax appears in the tooltip; preserve it verbatim.
publisher.markdownToolbar.bold.aria: ARIA label for the Bold button. Wraps the selection in `**`.
publisher.markdownToolbar.bold.title: Hover tooltip on the Bold button. Preserve the literal `**` markdown syntax in the tooltip.
publisher.markdownToolbar.codeBlock.aria: ARIA label for the Code-block button. Wraps the selection in triple-backtick code fences.
publisher.markdownToolbar.codeBlock.titl: Hover tooltip on the Code-block button. Preserve the literal ``` fence syntax.
publisher.markdownToolbar.codeInline.ari: ARIA label for the Inline-code button. Wraps the selection in single backticks.
publisher.markdownToolbar.codeInline.tit: Hover tooltip on the Inline-code button. Preserve the literal backtick character.
publisher.markdownToolbar.h2.aria: ARIA label for the Heading button. Prepends `## ` to each selected line. (H1 isn't in the markdown allowlist; H2 is the largest available.)
publisher.markdownToolbar.h2.title: Hover tooltip on the Heading button. Preserve the literal `##` markdown syntax.
publisher.markdownToolbar.italic.aria: ARIA label for the Italic button. Wraps the selection in `*`.
publisher.markdownToolbar.italic.title: Hover tooltip on the Italic button. Preserve the literal `*` markdown syntax.
publisher.markdownToolbar.link.aria: ARIA label for the Link button. Inserts the `[text](url)` markdown.
publisher.markdownToolbar.link.title: Hover tooltip on the Link button. Preserve the literal `[text](url)` markdown shape.
publisher.markdownToolbar.listBullet.ari: ARIA label for the Bullet-list button. Prepends `- ` to each selected line.
publisher.markdownToolbar.listBullet.tit: Hover tooltip on the Bullet-list button. Preserve the literal `-` markdown syntax.
publisher.markdownToolbar.listNumbered.a: ARIA label for the Numbered-list button. Prepends `1. ` to each selected line.
publisher.markdownToolbar.listNumbered.t: Hover tooltip on the Numbered-list button. Preserve the literal `1.` markdown syntax.
publisher.me.affiliation.none: Shown in the Affiliation row of the publisher's profile card when the publisher hasn't set an institution / organisation yet. Brief — fits in the value column.
publisher.me.error.network: Error message shown on the profile page when the fetch to /api/v1/publish/me throws (no network, DNS failure, etc.). Paired with a Refresh button.
publisher.me.error.refresh: Label for the refresh button shown alongside every error state on the profile page. Single word; the button reloads the page so Cloudflare Access can re-issue an identity token if needed.
publisher.me.error.server: Error message shown on the profile page when the server returns a 5xx. Suggests retrying soon; the user typically can't fix this themselves.
publisher.me.error.session: Error message shown on the profile page when the publisher API is gated by Cloudflare Access but the user's API-scoped cookie is missing or expired (either a fresh visit where they signed into the portal app but not the API app, or a real expiry). Paired with the Sign in button that runs them through the redirect-back endpoint to set the API cookie. See `docs/SELF_HOSTING.md` §8f for the two-app cookie-scope context.
publisher.me.error.signIn: Label for the Sign in button shown alongside the session-expired error on the profile page. Triggers a top-level navigation to /api/v1/publish/redirect-back so Cloudflare Access can set the API app's cookie before bouncing the user back to /publish/me.
publisher.me.field.affiliation: Label for the Affiliation row of the publisher's profile card. Sits to the left of the value (e.g., 'NOAA/PMEL').
publisher.me.field.email: Label for the Email row of the publisher's profile card. Sits to the left of the value (the publisher's verified email address).
publisher.me.field.memberSince: Label for the Member-since row of the publisher's profile card. Value is a localized date — formatted by Intl.DateTimeFormat with year + month + day.
publisher.me.field.role: Label for the Role row of the publisher's profile card. Sits to the left of one or two badges showing the publisher's role (Staff / Community / Service / Read-only) and an optional Admin badge.
publisher.me.field.status: Label for the Status row of the publisher's profile card. Value is a coloured badge (Active / Pending approval / Suspended).
publisher.me.heading: H2 heading at the top of the publisher profile card (/publish/me). Internal context: only the publisher viewing it sees this string.
publisher.me.loading: Live-region status text shown while the profile fetch is in flight. Should imply work-in-progress, brief.
publisher.me.role.admin: Role badge text for admin publishers — full administrative authority over the deploying node's catalog, including approving and managing other publishers. Keep short — fits inside a badge.
publisher.me.role.publisher: Role badge text for publisher accounts — the secondary authoring role that can publish and edit its own datasets but not manage other users. Keep short — fits inside a badge.
publisher.me.role.readonly: Role badge text for read-only publishers (auditors, assigned reviewers without write access). Keep short — fits inside a badge.
publisher.me.role.service: Role badge text for non-interactive service accounts (CI pipelines, scheduled jobs using a Cloudflare Access service token). Keep short.
publisher.me.status.active: Status badge text for an active publisher (can publish, edit, retract). Keep short — fits inside a badge.
publisher.me.status.pending: Status badge text for a publisher account whose row exists but hasn't been approved yet (publisher accounts default to this; an admin approves them in the portal's Users tab). 'Pending approval' is two words — keep it tight.
publisher.me.status.suspended: Status badge text for a suspended publisher. The account exists but cannot write; this is a reversible admin action distinct from deletion. Keep short.
publisher.nav.aria: ARIA label for the publisher portal's top nav (the `<nav>` element holding the section tabs). Read by screen readers when the user enters the nav; not visible text.
publisher.nav.backToTerraviz: ARIA label for the back arrow in the portal topbar — the publisher's escape hatch from the portal back to the main Terraviz SPA. The visible character is '←'; this label is read by screen readers.
publisher.nav.datasets: Visible label for the Datasets tab in the portal's top nav. Same surface as `publisher.section.datasets` — kept as a separate key to give translators latitude over tab length (a tab may need a shorter word than a section heading in some languages).
publisher.nav.import: Visible label for the Import tab in the portal's top nav. Keep short — it sits alongside three other tabs.
publisher.nav.profile: Visible label for the Profile tab in the portal's top nav. Routes to /publish/me. Same surface as `publisher.section.profile` — separate key for tab-length flexibility.
publisher.nav.signOut: Label for the Sign out link in the portal's top nav. Navigates to /api/v1/logout, which redirects through Cloudflare Access's team-level logout endpoint, which clears the user's Access session and bounces them back to the public app at /. Should read as a deliberate action, not a tab.
publisher.nav.tours: Visible label for the Tours tab in the portal's top nav. Same surface as `publisher.section.tours` — separate key for tab-length flexibility.
publisher.placeholder.comingSoon: Placeholder line shown on portal pages that haven't shipped yet. Preserve {subPhase} verbatim — it is the sub-phase tag (e.g., '3pb', '3pe') and not localizable. Internal context, not seen by end users; only the small group of staff publishers see this string and only during the rollout window.
publisher.portal.title: Top-level h1 on every page of the publisher portal — the admin UI where staff create and manage Terraviz datasets and tours. Not visible to end users; only visible to authenticated publishers.
publisher.section.datasetDetail: Heading for a single dataset's edit / view page in the publisher portal. Preserve {id} verbatim — it is the dataset's ULID identifier and not localizable.
publisher.section.datasets: Section heading and nav label for the dataset-management area of the publisher portal. Short — also used as a nav tab.
publisher.section.import: Section heading for the bulk-import area where publishers drop a CSV or JSON to create many dataset rows at once. 'Bulk' clarifies that this is not a single-dataset action.
publisher.section.notFound: Heading shown when a publisher navigates to a /publish/* URL that doesn't match any portal route. Brief, neutral; not an error condition.
publisher.section.profile: Section heading and nav label for the publisher's own profile page (/publish/me) — shows email, role, affiliation. Short — also used as a nav tab.
publisher.section.tours: Section heading and nav label for the tour-management area of the publisher portal. Short — also used as a nav tab.
publisher.users.confirm.reject: Confirm dialog text before an admin rejects a pending account. {name} is the account's display name — keep the placeholder verbatim.
publisher.users.confirm.suspend: Confirm dialog text before an admin suspends an active account. {name} is the account's display name — keep the placeholder verbatim.
publisher.users.restricted: Shown on the Users tab to a non-admin who reaches it directly. Explains the tab is admin-only.
tour.image.alt: Alt text for tour-embedded images. Describes the image to screen-reader users; not visible text on screen.
tour.legend.alt: Alt text for tour legend images. Describes the legend; not visible text.
tour.question.alt: Alt text for tour question icons. Indicates this is a question prompt; not visible text.
tour.question.answer.alt: Alt text for tour answer icons. Indicates this is an answer; not visible text.
tour.question.answer.aria: ARIA label for tour answer buttons. {n} is the answer number (1, 2, 3…). Read aloud by screen readers when the button is focused; not visible text.
tour.question.loading: Placeholder text shown briefly on the VR tour-question overlay while the question image is still being fetched. Keep short — drawn into a fixed canvas.
vr.controllerTooltip.grip: Floating tooltip above the user's VR controllers indicating what the grip button does. Keep short — drawn into a fixed-size canvas above each hand. 'Grip' refers to the squeeze button on the side of a VR controller.
vr.controllerTooltip.thumbstick: Floating tooltip above the user's VR controllers indicating what the thumbstick does. Keep short — drawn into a fixed-size canvas above each hand.
vr.controllerTooltip.trigger: Floating tooltip above the user's VR controllers indicating what the trigger does. Keep short — drawn into a fixed-size canvas above each hand. 'Trigger' refers to the index-finger button on a VR controller.
vr.zoomSlider.aria: ARIA label for the in-AR vertical zoom slider on phones. Describes its function for screen readers; not visible text.
vr.zoomSlider.label: Visible label printed above the in-AR zoom slider on phones. Single short word — the column it sits in is narrow.
vr.zoomSlider.title: Tooltip on the in-AR zoom slider when hovered with a pointer. Brief — appears as a native browser tooltip.
